title: Intoduction
text: AgreementPhi package exports utility function simulate data providing true agreement item effects. Consider example simulate continuous ratings 200 items, collecting 8 relevance assessments per item, total 1600 responses. set true agreement Φ=0.4\Phi=0.4 simulated 1600 ratings stored dt$ratings, dt$id_item dt$id_worker store item worker indices related rating convenience, also provide plot utility visualise data  core function AgreementPhi package agreement(), implements numerical algorithms estimate Φ\Phi agreement via profile modified profile likelihood methods. requires input ratings, item ids worker ids (specified integers). estimation via profile likelihood, can specify METHOD="profile" VERBOSE option chosen, function prints screen useful information data dimensions sparsity. addition, also provides overview items worker effects treated. case, example, worker effects considered constant (set zero default), items effects profiled nuisance parameters. agreement precision estimates available maximum likelihood estimates nuisance parameters available use modified likelihood approach, enough change METHOD argument modified. can read verbose output, METHOD = "modified", proposed algorithm first optimises profile likelihood evaluate maximum likelihood estimators needed construct modified profile likelihood. Thus, estimates can retrieved fitted object point estimates computed, can draw inference agreement using get_ci() function construct confidence intervals. function automatically recognise estimates related profile modified likelihood approach looking fitted object convenience, provide utility function visualise relative log-likelihood profiles two methods well inference results
code: library(AgreementPhi) set.seed(321) # setting dimension items <- 200 budget_per_item <- 8 n_obs <- items * budget_per_item  # item-specific intercepts to generate the data alphas <- runif(items, -1, 1)  # true agreement (between 0 and 1) agr <- .4  # generate continuous rating in (0,1) dt <- sim_data(   J = items,   B = budget_per_item,   AGREEMENT = agr,   DATA_TYPE = "continuous",   ALPHA = alphas ) names(dt) #> [1] "id_item"   "id_worker" "rating" head(dt$id_item) #> [1] 20 44 45 51 83 92 head(dt$id_worker) #> [1] 1 1 1 1 1 1 head(dt$rating) #> [1] 0.6375834 0.7420828 0.9866412 0.4995354 0.2111970 0.0271688 length(dt$rating) #> [1] 1600 plot_data(   RATINGS = dt$rating,   ITEM_INDS = dt$id_item,   WORKER_INDS = dt$id_worker ) # estimation via profile likelihood fit_profile <- agreement(   RATINGS = dt$rating,   ITEM_INDS = dt$id_item,   WORKER_INDS = dt$id_worker,   NUISANCE = c("items"),   METHOD = "profile",   VERBOSE = TRUE) #>  #> DATA #>  - Detected 200 items and 200 workers. #>  - Detected continuous data on the (0,1) range. #>  - Average number of observed ratings per item is 8. #>  - Average number of observed ratings per worker is 8. #>  #> MODEL PARAMETERS #>  - Constant effects: workers #>  - Nuisance effects: items #> Done! fit_profile$profile #> $precision #> [1] 2.446563 #>  #> $agreement #> [1] 0.4444125 head(fit_profile$alpha) #> [1]  1.0698711  1.4333241 -0.2898244 -0.9135686  0.3879414  0.1008767 # estimation via modified profile likelihood fit_modified <- agreement(   RATINGS = dt$rating,   ITEM_INDS = dt$id_item,   WORKER_INDS = dt$id_worker,   NUISANCE = c("items"),   METHOD = "modified",   VERBOSE = TRUE) #>  #> DATA #>  - Detected 200 items and 200 workers. #>  - Detected continuous data on the (0,1) range. #>  - Average number of observed ratings per item is 8. #>  - Average number of observed ratings per worker is 8. #>  #> MODEL PARAMETERS #>  - Constant effects: workers #>  - Nuisance effects: items #> Non-adjusted agreement: 0.444413 #> Adjusted agreement: 0.400506 #> Done! fit_modified$profile #> $precision #> [1] 2.446563 #>  #> $agreement #> [1] 0.4444125 fit_modified$modified #> $precision #> [1] 2.129948 #>  #> $agreement #> [1] 0.4005064 # get standard error and confidence interval ci_profile <- get_ci(fit_profile) ci_profile #> $agreement_est #> [1] 0.4444125 #>  #> $agreement_se #> [1] 0.0102727 #>  #> $agreement_ci #> [1] 0.4242784 0.4645467  ci_modified <- get_ci(fit_modified) ci_modified  #> $agreement_est #> [1] 0.4005064 #>  #> $agreement_se #> [1] 0.0103424 #>  #> $agreement_ci #> [1] 0.3802357 0.4207772 # compute log-likelihood over a grid range_ll <- get_range_ll(fit_modified)  # utility plot function for relative log-likelihood plot_rll(   D=range_ll,   M_EST = fit_modified$modified$agreement,   P_EST = fit_profile$profile$agreement,   M_SE = ci_modified$agreement_se,   P_SE = ci_profile$agreement_se,   CONFIDENCE=.95 )

title: General Agreement Phi
text: AgreementPhi package companion “Alfonzetti G., Bellio R., Vidoni P. Accurate agreement estimation crowdsourced relevance assessments”. allows accurate estimation general Φ\Phi agreement measure among multiple crowd-workers assessing given collection items (Checco et al. 2017).

title: General Agreement Phi
text: can install AgreementPhi using devtools package:
code: devtools::install_github("giuseppealfonzetti/AgreementPhi")

title: General Agreement Phi
text: Generate synthetic dataset continuous ratings (0,1) Fit model using agreement() function Construct confidence intervals
code: library(AgreementPhi) set.seed(321) # setting dimension items <- 200 budget_per_item <- 8 n_obs <- items * budget_per_item  # item-specific intercepts to generate the data alphas <- runif(items, -2, 2)  # true agreement (between 0 and 1) agr <- .8  # generate continuous rating in (0,1) dt <- sim_data(   J = items,   B = budget_per_item,   AGREEMENT = agr,   DATA_TYPE = "continuous",   ALPHA = alphas ) # estimation via modified profile likelihood fit <- agreement(   RATINGS = dt$rating,   ITEM_INDS = dt$id_item,   WORKER_INDS = dt$id_worker,   METHOD = "modified",   NUISANCE = c("items"),   VERBOSE = TRUE) #>  #> DATA #>  - Detected 200 items and 200 workers. #>  - Detected continuous data on the (0,1) range. #>  - Average number of observed ratings per item is 8. #>  - Average number of observed ratings per worker is 8. #>  #> MODEL PARAMETERS #>  - Constant effects: workers #>  - Nuisance effects: items #> Non-adjusted agreement: 0.835331 #> Adjusted agreement: 0.792021 #> Done! # get standard error and confidence interval ci <- get_ci(fit) ci  #> $agreement_est #> [1] 0.7920213 #>  #> $agreement_se #> [1] 0.01203436 #>  #> $agreement_ci #> [1] 0.7684344 0.8156082

title: From agreement to precision — agr2prec

title: Compute Agreement — agreement
text: Compute \(\Phi\) agreement proposed Checco et al. (2017) via profile likelihood methods.

title: Compute Agreement — agreement
code: agreement(   RATINGS,   ITEM_INDS,   WORKER_INDS = NULL,   METHOD = c("modified", "profile"),   ALPHA_START = NULL,   BETA_START = NULL,   TAU = NULL,   PHI_START = NULL,   NUISANCE = c("items", "workers"),   CONTROL = list(),   VERBOSE = FALSE )

title: Compute Agreement — agreement
text: RATINGS Ratings vector dimension n. Ordinal data must coded {1, 2, ..., K}. Continuous data can take values (0, 1). ITEM_INDS Index vector items allocations. dimension RATINGS. WORKER_INDS Index vector worker allocations. dimension RATINGS. Ignored MODEL == "oneway". Must integers {1, 2, ..., J}. METHOD Choose "modified" "profile". Default "modified". "modified": Uses modified profile likelihood Barndorff-Nielsen correction "profile": Uses standard profile likelihood ALPHA_START Starting values item-specific intercepts. Vector length J. Default rep(0, J) J number items. BETA_START Starting values worker-specific intercepts. Vector length W-1. Default rep(0, W-1) W number workers TAU Thresholds use discretisation underlying beta distribution. PHI_START Starting value beta precision parameter. Must positive. Default agr2prec(0.5) (precision corresponding 50% agreement). NUISANCE Vector containg either "items" "workers" . Defines fixed effects profile estimation. CONTROL Control options optimization: SEARCH_RANGE Search range precision parameter optimization. algorithm searches [1e-8, PHI_START + SEARCH_RANGE]. Must positive. Default: 8. MAX_ITER Maximum number iterations precision parameter optimization. Must positive integer. Default: 100. PROF_SEARCH_RANGE Search range profiling nuisance parameters (item intercepts). algorithm searches [ALPHA_START[j] - PROF_SEARCH_RANGE, ALPHA_START[j] + PROF_SEARCH_RANGE] item j. Must positive. Default: 4. PROF_MAX_ITER Maximum number iterations profiling optimization. Must positive integer. Default: 10. ALT_MAX_ITER Maximum iterations alternating profiling. Must positive integer. Default: 10. ALT_TOL Relative convergence tolerance alternating profiling. Must positive. Default: 1e-2. VERBOSE Verbose output.

title: Compute Agreement — agreement
text: Returns list maximum likelihood estimates corresponding negative log-likelihood.

title: Compute Agreement — agreement
text: Checco ., Roitero K., Maddalena E., Mizzaro S., Demartini G., (2017). “Let’s Agree Disagree: Fixing Agreement Measures Crowdsourcing.” Proceedings AAAI Conference Human Computation Crowdsourcing 5: 11–20. doi

title: Compute Agreement — agreement
code: set.seed(321)  # setting dimension items <- 50 budget_per_item <- 5 n_obs <- items * budget_per_item workers <- 50  # item-specific intercepts to generate the data alphas <- runif(items, -2, 2)  # true agreement (between 0 and 1) agr <- .6  # generate continuous rating in (0,1) dt_oneway <- sim_data(   J = items,   B = budget_per_item,   AGREEMENT = agr,   ALPHA = alphas,   DATA_TYPE = "continuous",   SEED = 123 )  # estimation via oneway specification fit <- agreement(   RATINGS = dt_oneway$rating,   ITEM_INDS = dt_oneway$id_item,   WORKER_INDS = dt_oneway$id_worker,   METHOD = "modified",   NUISANCE = c("items"),   VERBOSE = TRUE ) #>  #> DATA #>  - Detected 50 items and 49 workers. #>  - Detected continuous data on the (0,1) range. #>  - Average number of observed ratings per item is 5. #>  - Average number of observed ratings per worker is 5.1. #>  #> MODEL PARAMETERS #>  - Constant effects: workers #>  - Nuisance effects: items #> Non-adjusted agreement: 0.739811 #> Adjusted agreement: 0.657685 #> Done! # get standard error and confidence interval ci <- get_ci(fit) ci #> $agreement_est #> [1] 0.6576846 #>  #> $agreement_se #> [1] 0.03584845 #>  #> $agreement_ci #> [1] 0.5874229 0.7279463 #>   # compute log-likelihood over a grid range_ll <- get_range_ll(fit)  # utility plot function for relative log-likelihood plot_rll(   D = range_ll,   M_EST = fit$modified$agreement,   P_EST = fit$profile$agreement,   M_SE = ci$agreement_se,   CONFIDENCE=.95 )

WORKER_INDS Index vector worker allocations. dimension RATINGS. Ignored MODEL == "oneway". Must integers {1, 2, ..., J}. VERBOSE Verbose output.

title: Plot data — plot_data
code: set.seed(321)  # setting dimension items <- 50 budget_per_item <- 5 n_obs <- items * budget_per_item  # item-specific intercepts to generate the data alphas <- runif(items, -2, 2)  # true agreement (between 0 and 1) agr <- .6  # generate continuous rating in (0,1) dt <- sim_data(   J = items,   B = budget_per_item,   AGREEMENT = agr,   ALPHA = alphas,   DATA_TYPE = "continuous",   SEED = 123 )  plot_data(   RATINGS = dt$rating,   ITEM_INDS = dt$id_item,   WORKER_INDS = dt$id_worker )

text: D output get_range_ll M_EST agreement estimate modified profile likelihood P_EST agreement estimate profile likelihood M_SE standard error agreement estimate modified profile likelihood P_SE standard error agreement estimate profile likelihood CONFIDENCE Confidence level construct confidence intervals

title: Plot relative log-likelihood — plot_rll
code: set.seed(321)  # setting dimension items <- 50 budget_per_item <- 5 n_obs <- items * budget_per_item workers <- 50  # item-specific intercepts to generate the data alphas <- runif(items, -2, 2)  # true agreement (between 0 and 1) agr <- .6  # generate continuous rating in (0,1) dt_oneway <- sim_data(   J = items,   B = budget_per_item,   AGREEMENT = agr,   ALPHA = alphas,   DATA_TYPE = "continuous",   SEED = 123 )  # estimation via oneway specification fit <- agreement(   RATINGS = dt_oneway$rating,   ITEM_INDS = dt_oneway$id_item,   WORKER_INDS = dt_oneway$id_worker,   METHOD = "modified",   NUISANCE = c("items"),   VERBOSE = TRUE ) #>  #> DATA #>  - Detected 50 items and 49 workers. #>  - Detected continuous data on the (0,1) range. #>  - Average number of observed ratings per item is 5. #>  - Average number of observed ratings per worker is 5.1. #>  #> MODEL PARAMETERS #>  - Constant effects: workers #>  - Nuisance effects: items #> Non-adjusted agreement: 0.739811 #> Adjusted agreement: 0.657685 #> Done! # get standard error and confidence interval ci <- get_ci(fit) ci #> $agreement_est #> [1] 0.6576846 #>  #> $agreement_se #> [1] 0.03584845 #>  #> $agreement_ci #> [1] 0.5874229 0.7279463 #>   # compute log-likelihood over a grid range_ll <- get_range_ll(fit)  # utility plot function for relative log-likelihood plot_rll(   D = range_ll,   M_EST = fit$modified$agreement,   P_EST = fit$profile$agreement,   M_SE = ci$agreement_se,   CONFIDENCE=.95 )

title: Simulate ordinal or continuous (0,1) ratings — sim_data
code: sim_data(   J,   B,   W = J,   AGREEMENT,   ALPHA,   BETA = NULL,   DATA_TYPE = c("ordinal", "continuous"),   K = 6,   SEED = 123 )

title: Simulate ordinal or continuous (0,1) ratings — sim_data
text: J Number items. B Budget per item (.e. number workers assigned item). W Maximum number workers. AGREEMENT General agreement. ALPHA Item-specific intercepts. BETA Worker-specific intercepts. DATA_TYPE Choose ordinal continuous. K Number categories case ordinal data. SEED RNG seed.

title: Simulate ordinal or continuous (0,1) ratings — sim_data
text: Returns dataframe columns id_items, id_worker rating

title: Simulate ordinal or continuous (0,1) ratings — sim_data
code: set.seed(123)  # generate from one-way model # (varying item effects, worker effects fixed to zero) dt1way <- sim_data(  J = 50,  B = 5,  AGREEMENT = .8,  ALPHA = runif(50, 0, 1),  DATA_TYPE = "continuous",  SEED = 123 ) # generate from two-way model # (varying item effects, varying worker effects) dt2way <- sim_data(  J = 50,  W = 40,  B = 5,  AGREEMENT = .8,  ALPHA = runif(50, 0, 1),  BETA = runif(40, 0, 1),  DATA_TYPE = "continuous",  SEED = 123 )
